gap_summaries.title: progressive_delivery not in VALID_SYSTEM_CATEGORIES
gap_summaries.description: Profile validator (_validate_categories) rejects unknown system categories. progressive_delivery was not in VALID_SYSTEM_CATEGORIES, so any profile listing it would fail validation. product_profile.py was marked Untouched.
gap_summaries.fix: Moved product_profile.py to Modified list. Added progressive_delivery to VALID_SYSTEM_CATEGORIES in Task 12 step 3b.

gap_summaries.title: Gate file tier persisted as 'code' instead of 'system'
gap_summaries.description: evaluate_gate -> build_gate_file -> make_gate_file(tier='code'). The plan called evaluate_gate then mutated gate_file['tier'] = 'system' after, but evaluate_gate persists the gate file internally with tier='code'. The persisted artifact is wrong.
gap_summaries.fix: Added backward-compatible tier: str = 'code' parameter to build_gate_file and evaluate_gate in verdict_engine.py. system_gate.py now passes tier='system'. Moved verdict_engine.py to Modified list.

gap_summaries.title: No handling when environment provisioning fails
gap_summaries.description: provision_system_env returns SystemEnvInfo(provisioned=False) on failure. run_system_gate would proceed to run collectors against a non-existent environment, all of which would timeout/error. Wasteful and misleading errors.
gap_summaries.fix: Added early-exit in run_system_gate: if env_info.provisioned is False, return FAIL gate file immediately with _provision_failed flag. Added test_provision_failure_returns_fail test.

gap_summaries.title: Evidence path collision between code and system gates
gap_summaries.description: Code and system gates share _bmad/gate/evidence/<gate_id>/. If same gate_id used for both, evidence records collide. Mitigated by convention: system gates use distinct gate IDs (e.g., 'sg-*').

gap_summaries.title: Shared crash marker between code and system gates
gap_summaries.description: gate-in-progress.json is shared. Concurrent code + system gates would conflict. Gate execution is sequential by design, so this is low-risk.

gap_summaries.title: Unnecessary git checkout for system collectors
gap_summaries.description: run_gate_collectors wraps collection in collector_checkout (fresh git checkout at SHA). System collectors check a live K8s cluster, not the codebase. The checkout is wasted I/O but harmless.

gap_summaries.title: Missing system-altitude timeout defaults
gap_summaries.description: DEFAULT_TIMEOUTS only had code categories (security: 300, etc.). System categories fell back to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK = 120s. k6 load tests and chaos experiments need 600-900s.
gap_summaries.fix: Added system timeout defaults in Task 12 step 3b: reliability: 600, resilience: 900, durable_hitl: 600, blast_radius: 900, cost_to_serve: 900, progressive_delivery: 300.

gap_summaries.title: resilience_rule zero-scenario edge case
gap_summaries.description: If scenarios_total=0 and scenarios_passed=0, the condition 'total > 0 and passed < total' is False, so the rule returns PASS. Zero scenarios means no resilience testing occurred.
gap_summaries.fix: Added 'if total == 0: return FAIL(no resilience scenarios executed)' before the passed < total check. Added test_fail_zero_scenarios test.

gap_summaries.title: Bool coercion on metric values (slo_breached, signal_survived)
gap_summaries.description: blast_radius_rule uses bool(_aggregate_metrics(evidence, 'slo_breached', False)). If the metric is stored as string 'false' from JSON, bool('false') == True. Mitigated: JSON false -> Python False natively.

gap_summaries.title: Fragile epic ID filtering in CLI (startswith 'E')
gap_summaries.description: gate_system_status_action filtered parked items with story_key.startswith('E'). This is fragile and semantically wrong.
gap_summaries.fix: Replaced with filter on reason='exhausted' or tier='system' in the parked data.

gap_summaries.title: progressive_delivery not in default profile categories.system
gap_summaries.description: Even with progressive_delivery in VALID_SYSTEM_CATEGORIES, existing profiles don't list it. By design: progressive_delivery is opt-in (not all epics use Argo Rollouts).

gap_summaries.title: No test asserting _runtime_env NOT in persisted gate file
gap_summaries.description: Tests verify _runtime_env IS injected into the profile copy for collectors, but don't verify it's absent from the persisted gate file. Safe by design since evaluate_gate receives the original profile.

gap_summaries.title: No violation status tests for system rules
gap_summaries.description: Rule functions handle violation status explicitly (FAIL on violation), but no dedicated tests. Fail-closed behavior is tested via error/timeout paths. Zero-scenario test adds coverage.

gap_summaries.title: UUID4 non-determinism in env_id
Hardcoded chaos YAML paths in resilience collectors
Missing tool_version_cmd on some collectors
stories_to_reopen returns ALL stories on FAIL
Test fixture duplication across test files
HR6(e/g) compliance/observability already code-altitude
CLAUDE.md module map update needed for new modules
park_story uses epic_id as story_key (semantic confusion)
deepcopy performance cost in _inject_runtime_env
Teardown error swallowing in teardown_system_env
